title: How Pansies Or Hearts-Ease Came First
content:
Frolic virgins once these were,
Overloving, living here;
Being here their ends denied
Ran for sweet-hearts mad, and died.
Love, in pity of their tears,
And their loss in blooming years,
For their restless here-spent hours,
Gave them hearts-ease turn'd to flowers.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Robert Herrick delivers a powerful performance in "How Pansies Or Hearts-Ease Came First"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
